qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated SQL injection in User_Location plugin regions endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding reports SQL injection in plugin/User_Location/regions.json.php, while the advisory describes command injection in plugin/Live/test.php. These are different vulnerability types (CWE-89 vs CWE-78), different vulnerable functions (IP2Location::getRegions() SQL concatenation vs wget() shell command injection), different code paths, and different root causes. No code overlap or shared vulnerable pattern exists between the finding and the advisory-described vulnerability.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes command injection (CWE-78) in plugin/Live/test.php via the wget() function where unsanitized URL and filename parameters are passed to exec(). The finding reports SQL injection (CWE-89) in plugin/User_Location/regions.json.php via IP2Location::getRegions() where the country parameter is concatenated into SQL without parameterized binding. These are entirely different vulnerabilities in different components with different root causes and attack vectors.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-pq8p-wc4f-vg7j describes command injection in plugin/Live/test.php wget() function with unsanitized exec() call
Finding sqli_user_location_regions reports SQL injection in plugin/User_Location/regions.json.php via IP2Location::getRegions()
No code overlap between plugin/Live/test.php and plugin/User_Location/regions.json.php or IP2Location.php
Different CWE classifications: CWE-78 (OS Command Injection) vs CWE-89 (SQL Injection)
Different vulnerable sinks: exec() for shell commands vs mysqli::prepare() for SQL queries
Different attack vectors: URL parameter for command injection vs country parameter for SQL injection
